No `pkgver()` function is defined, so no custom logic runs to alter the package version at parse time. The top-level content consists only of standard metadata assignments and arrays, which are inert. As such, the operations triggered by the two specified `makepkg` commands are safe and limited to cloning a public, signed git tag from the legitimate upstream, with no exfiltration, injection, or remote execution risk.
